9	// Retry policy logical outcome names. These are the policy's semantic
10	// vocabulary, distinct from execution status and failure-mode constants.
11	const (
12		// RetryOutcomeSuccess marks a successful merge; no retry needed.
13		RetryOutcomeSuccess = "success"
14	
15		// RetryOutcomeNoChangesAfterAttempt marks an attempt where the agent
16		// produced no commits. Retryable with a higher MinPower.
17		RetryOutcomeNoChangesAfterAttempt = "no_changes_after_attempt"
18	
19		// RetryOutcomeReviewBlockedCapability marks an attempt where the
20		// post-merge reviewer returned BLOCK or REQUEST_CHANGES, indicating the
21		// model's output did not meet the acceptance criteria. Retryable with a
22		// higher MinPower.
23		RetryOutcomeReviewBlockedCapability = "review_blocked_capability"
24	
25		// RetryOutcomePassthroughExhausted marks an attempt where the passthrough
26		// envelope conflicts with the power bounds (blocked_by_passthrough_constraint
27		// or agent_power_unsatisfied). Power escalation cannot resolve this;
28		// the policy stops instead of mutating the passthrough.
29		RetryOutcomePassthroughExhausted = "passthrough_exhausted"
30	
31		// RetryOutcomeSetupFailure marks a deterministic infrastructure failure that
32		// power escalation cannot fix: harness not installed, no viable provider,
33		// auth error, etc. The policy stops.
34		RetryOutcomeSetupFailure = "deterministic_setup_failure"
35	
36		// RetryOutcomeMaxPowerNoProgress marks an attempt where MinPower cannot be
37		// raised any further — either because the next threshold would exceed
38		// MaxPower, or because the catalog has no higher power tier available.
39		// The policy stops.
40		RetryOutcomeMaxPowerNoProgress = "max_power_no_progress"
41	
42		// RetryOutcomeOther marks any outcome not eligible for power escalation.
43		RetryOutcomeOther = "other"
44	)
45	
46	// RetryDecision is the policy output for one bead attempt outcome.
47	// It never includes DDx-selected harness, provider, model, or model-ref pins;
48	// only a MinPower adjustment is returned.
49	type RetryDecision struct {
50		// ShouldRetry indicates whether the bead should be retried with escalated
51		// MinPower. false means the policy decided to stop.
52		ShouldRetry bool
53	
54		// NextMinPower is the MinPower to use for the next attempt.
55		// Only meaningful when ShouldRetry is true.
56		NextMinPower int
57	
58		// Reason classifies why the policy decided to retry or stop.
59		// Populated for both outcomes and used in audit records.
60		Reason string
61	
62		// Evidence provides additional context for the audit record.
63		Evidence string
64	}
65	
66	// ClassifyRetryOutcome maps an attempt's status and failure_mode into a
67	// logical retry outcome that EvaluateRetryPolicy uses to make its decision.
68	//
69	// Passthrough exhaustion (blocked_by_passthrough_constraint or
70	// agent_power_unsatisfied) is detected first and always results in a stop,
71	// regardless of the status string. Deterministic setup failures (harness not
72	// installed, no viable provider, auth error) are detected next. All other
73	// classification is done from status alone.
74	func ClassifyRetryOutcome(status, failureMode string) string {
75		// Passthrough exhaustion stops regardless of other signals; inspecting
76		// the failure mode is sufficient and does not require reading string values
77		// from the passthrough envelope itself.
78		switch failureMode {
79		case FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint, FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied:
80			return RetryOutcomePassthroughExhausted
81		}
82	
83		// Deterministic setup failures that power escalation cannot fix.
84		switch failureMode {
85		case FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled, FailureModeNoViableProvider, FailureModeAuthError:
86			return RetryOutcomeSetupFailure
87		}
88	
89		switch status {
90		case ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
91			return RetryOutcomeSuccess
92		case ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges:
93			return RetryOutcomeNoChangesAfterAttempt
94		case ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock, ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges,
95			ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction:
96			return RetryOutcomeReviewBlockedCapability
97		}
98	
99		return RetryOutcomeOther
100	}
101	
102	// EvaluateRetryPolicy makes the retry/stop decision for one bead attempt.
103	//
104	// outcome must come from ClassifyRetryOutcome. actualPower is the power level
105	// used in the prior attempt (from routing evidence's actual_power field).
106	// currentMinPower and maxPower are the bounds currently in effect for the bead.
107	// models is the agent model catalog; when non-empty, top-1 (the strongest tier)
108	// is used as the escalation target.
109	//
110	// The returned RetryDecision never includes DDx-selected harness, provider,
111	// model, or model-ref pins — only a new MinPower bound. The passthrough
112	// envelope is neither read nor mutated.
113	func EvaluateRetryPolicy(
114		outcome string,
115		actualPower int,
116		currentMinPower int,
117		maxPower int,
118		models []agentlib.ModelInfo,
119	) RetryDecision {
120		switch outcome {
121		case RetryOutcomeSuccess:
122			return RetryDecision{
123				ShouldRetry: false,
124				Reason:      RetryOutcomeSuccess,
125				Evidence:    "attempt succeeded; no retry needed",
126			}
127	
128		case RetryOutcomePassthroughExhausted:
129			return RetryDecision{
130				ShouldRetry: false,
131				Reason:      RetryOutcomePassthroughExhausted,
132				Evidence:    "passthrough constraint or agent power bounds incompatible; retry would not resolve",
133			}
134	
135		case RetryOutcomeSetupFailure:
136			return RetryDecision{
137				ShouldRetry: false,
138				Reason:      RetryOutcomeSetupFailure,
139				Evidence:    "deterministic setup failure; power escalation cannot fix",
140			}
141	
142		case RetryOutcomeNoChangesAfterAttempt, RetryOutcomeReviewBlockedCapability:
143			nextMinPower := computeNextMinPower(models, actualPower, currentMinPower)
144	
145			// MaxPower must be preserved across retries. If escalation would
146			// produce a NextMinPower that violates the MaxPower bound, stop.
147			if maxPower > 0 && nextMinPower > maxPower {
148				return RetryDecision{
149					ShouldRetry: false,
150					Reason:      RetryOutcomeMaxPowerNoProgress,
151					Evidence: fmt.Sprintf(
152						"next_min_power=%d would exceed max_power=%d; cannot escalate within bounds",
153						nextMinPower, maxPower),
154				}
155			}
156	
157			// If the computed next threshold is not higher than the power level
158			// the prior attempt already used, escalating to it would not constrain
159			// routing to a stronger model — the catalog has no higher tier.
160			if actualPower > 0 && nextMinPower <= actualPower {
161				return RetryDecision{
162					ShouldRetry: false,
163					Reason:      RetryOutcomeMaxPowerNoProgress,
164					Evidence: fmt.Sprintf(
165						"actual_power=%d; no higher power tier available in catalog",
166						actualPower),
167				}
168			}
169	
170			return RetryDecision{
171				ShouldRetry:  true,
172				NextMinPower: nextMinPower,
173				Reason:       outcome,
174				Evidence: fmt.Sprintf(
175					"actual_power=%d next_min_power=%d",
176					actualPower, nextMinPower),
177			}
178	
179		default:
180			return RetryDecision{
181				ShouldRetry: false,
182				Reason:      RetryOutcomeOther,
183				Evidence:    "outcome not eligible for power escalation: " + outcome,
184			}
185		}
186	}
187	
188	// computeNextMinPower returns the MinPower for the next escalated attempt.
189	// When the models catalog is non-empty, returns TopNPowerThreshold(models, 1)
190	// — the minimum power required to reach only the strongest model tier.
191	// When the catalog is empty, bumps above the highest observed power by a
192	// fixed delta.
193	func computeNextMinPower(models []agentlib.ModelInfo, actualPower, currentMinPower int) int {
194		top1 := TopNPowerThreshold(models, 1)
195		if top1 > 0 {
196			return top1
197		}
198		// No catalog: heuristic bump above the highest observed power.
199		base := currentMinPower
200		if actualPower > base {
201			base = actualPower
202		}
203		if base == 0 {
204			return 50
205		}
206		return base + 10
207	}

ID:       ddx-673833f4
Title:    TD: bead state machine — design + naming-role decisions (foundational doc)
Type:     task
Status:   closed

Desc:     Authoring-only sub-task of the bead state-machine work. Children (filed separately) handle the mechanical reconciliation, CI guards, and housekeeping that follow this design.

This bead OUTPUTS the canonical TD doc that the 5 hygiene beads + the 3 sibling reconciliation beads reference. NO code or schema changes in this bead — those are separate sub-beads.

## Critical constraint (per ADR-004)

The bead-record schema MUST stay compatible with the bd/br interchange contract. Status enum is FIXED at the bd/br canonical 6 (open, in_progress, closed, blocked, proposed, cancelled). DDx-specific execution semantics live in labels, events, or Extra map — NOT in new statuses.

## Today's actual state (verified)

- bead-record.schema.json enum: 6 values (the bd/br canonical set)
- FEAT-004 line 65 docs: 3 values (lags schema)
- Persisted reality: 'ddx bead list --json | jq -r .[].status | sort -u' shows only open/in_progress/closed in active rows today
- Code uses additional names (done, needs_human, pending, ready, review, needs_investigation) but these are NOT persisted statuses — event kinds, terminal phases, derived queue categories, or labels

## Five in-flight hygiene beads each touch state-machine vocabulary (must align with this TD)

- ddx-b24e9630 (no_changes verification): NoChangesContract subsection
- ddx-3c154349 (auto-triage): TriageContract subsection
- ddx-aede917d (drain pause on quota): QuotaPauseContract subsection
- ddx-c6e3db02 (rate-limit retry): RateLimitRetryContract subsection
- ddx-da11a34a (lock contention): LockContentionContract subsection

## Scope (this bead authors the doc only)

Author docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-NNN-bead-state-machine.md with:

1. Distinguish category of every observed name as one of: persisted bead status (locked to bd/br set) / derived queue category / event kind / terminal phase / claim metadata / label / Extra metadata field / worker state.
2. Persisted status enumeration — confirm the bd/br-compatible 6-value set. Status enum is NOT modified.
3. Transition matrix — table or diagram: which status → which is allowed; who can drive each (operator-via-CLI, drain loop, agent, reviewer); what events fire on each transition.
4. Event vocabulary — unified across hygiene beads (no_changes_*, drain-paused-quota, etc.).
5. Outcome → label/event/Extra mapping — when execute-bead returns merged/already_satisfied/review_block/execution_failed/no_changes_*, the resulting bead state changes (status transition within the 6, label add/remove, event append, Extra field update).
6. Claim semantics — when claimed, when released, stale claim handling.
7. Naming-role decision matrix — every observed name labeled with role + rationale: blocked, in_progress, open, closed, cancelled, proposed (current schema 6), done, pending, ready, review (in code as non-status), needs_human (label today; stays label), needs_investigation (LABEL per bd/br compatibility, NOT status), blocked-on-upstream:<id> (label).
8. Per-hygiene-bead contract subsection — one per bead (NoChangesContract, TriageContract, QuotaPauseContract, RateLimitRetryContract, LockContentionContract): status transitions used (within canonical 6), labels added/removed, events fired, claim behavior, loop interactions.
9. Future-change process — explicit rule: any bead introducing or changing labels/events/outcome semantics/claim handling MUST cite the TD section authorizing it. Adding a new persisted status requires upstream bd/br coordination + ADR-004 amendment.
10. Worker-state enumeration — parallel section: idle / draining / paused-quota / paused-rate-limit / exiting. Worker-state is distinct from bead-state, owned by worker process not bead store.
11. Migration plan placeholder — say 'sibling bead handles survey + execution' (the actual survey runs in the sibling bead).

## What does NOT land here

- Schema/code/docs reconciliation (sibling bead handles)
- CI guard tests (sibling bead handles)
- Hygiene-bead AC TD-NNN substitution + migration survey (sibling bead handles)
Accept:   1. docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-<NNN>-bead-state-machine.md exists with all 11 sections plus 5 per-hygiene-bead contract subsections. 2. depends_on: [FEAT-004, SD-004, ADR-004]. 3. Persisted status list is the bd/br-canonical 6 (no additions). 4. Naming-role decision matrix covers ALL observed names with rationale. 5. Future-change process rule explicit. 6. Worker-state enumeration parallel section present. 7. ddx doc audit clean for the new TD artifact.

348	### 8.4 RateLimitRetryContract (ddx-c6e3db02)
349	
350	Status transitions used: none. Rate-limit retry is internal to a single

353	Labels: none.

365	### 8.5 LockContentionContract (ddx-da11a34a)
366	
367	Status transitions used: none. Lock contention is a store-access
368	concern, not a bead-state concern.
369	
370	Labels: none.
371	
372	Events fired: `lock-contention` (append-only, on the affected bead, only
373	after the contention has been resolved and the mutation succeeded — so a
374	future audit can see that contention was observed). Pure read-side
375	contention does not emit an event.
376	
377	Claim behavior: unaffected. The claim is acquired or not; partial states
378	are not persisted.
379	
380	Loop interaction: the worker retries the locked operation with
381	exponential backoff up to a bounded budget. On budget exhaustion the
382	calling outcome maps to `execution_failed` (section 5). The worker does
383	not enter a dedicated worker-state for lock contention; it remains
384	`draining` and treats the failure as ordinary.

386	## 9. Future-Change Process
387	
388	This is the rule of record for any subsequent change to bead state.
389	
390	> Any bead that introduces or changes a label, an event kind, an outcome →
391	> state mapping, claim handling, or worker-state semantics MUST cite the
392	> TD-031 section that authorizes the change. If no section authorizes it,
393	> TD-031 is amended in the same PR (or a parent bead) before the dependent
394	> work lands.
395	>
396	> Adding a new persisted bead status is **not** authorized by TD-031.
397	> It requires upstream bd/br coordination AND an amendment to ADR-004.
398	
399	In practice:
400	
401	- A new event kind: amend section 4 in the same PR.
402	- A new outcome → state mapping: amend section 5.
403	- A new label: amend section 7.
404	- A new worker state: amend section 10.
405	- A new persisted status: do not start the work; file an ADR-004
406	  amendment first.
407	
408	CI guard: a sibling bead adds a check that any change to
409	`bead-record.schema.json` or to the persisted-status enum touches
410	ADR-004 and TD-031 in the same commit. That guard is out of scope for
411	this bead.
412	
413	## 10. Worker-State Enumeration
414	
415	Worker state is the in-process state of the drain loop. It is **distinct
416	from bead state** and is not persisted in the bead store. It exists only
417	for the worker's lifetime.
418	
419	| Worker state | Meaning | Entry | Exit |
420	|---|---|---|---|
421	| `idle` | Worker is up but not actively claiming. | Worker startup; transient between attempts. | Operator or scheduler moves it to `draining`. |
422	| `draining` | Worker is actively claiming and executing beads. | Default operating state. | Quota or rate-limit pause; explicit stop. |
423	| `paused-quota` | Worker has observed quota exhaustion on its harness. | `drain-paused-quota` event. | `drain-resumed-quota` event after backoff or operator resume. |
424	| `paused-rate-limit` | Worker is sleeping out a rate-limit retry window inside an attempt. | Rate-limit response from harness. | Wait window elapses; worker resumes the same attempt. |
425	| `exiting` | Worker is shutting down cleanly; will not claim more beads. | Operator stop or terminal failure. | Process exit. |
426	
427	Worker state is observable via the worker's stdout/log; it is not stored
428	on any bead. Hygiene beads that affect worker state (QuotaPauseContract,
429	RateLimitRetryContract) MUST NOT add new fields to the bead schema to
430	represent these transient states.
431	
432	## 11. Migration Plan (placeholder)
433	
434	The actual migration survey — which existing beads carry orphaned status
435	values, which code paths persist non-canonical statuses, and what the
436	mechanical rename looks like — runs in the sibling reconciliation bead.
437	This TD does not catalogue current data.
438	
439	Sibling beads handling the migration:

170	  rationale file
171	- `no_changes_recoverable` — transient cause, will retry
172	
173	Drain-control events:
174	
175	- `drain-paused-quota` — QuotaPauseContract: drain paused because the active
176	  harness reported quota exhaustion
177	- `drain-resumed-quota`
178	- `rate-limit-retry` — RateLimitRetryContract: a single attempt was retried
179	  after a rate-limit response
180	- `lock-contention` — LockContentionContract: store-lock contention was
181	  observed and handled (retry or backoff)
182	
183	Review and triage events:
184	
185	- `review-block` — reviewer raised a BLOCKING finding
186	- `review-pass` — reviewer cleared the change
187	- `auto-triage` — TriageContract: triage path mutated labels/status
188	
189	Each event SHOULD include `kind`, `actor`, `created_at`, and a free-form
190	`body` (TD-004 schema). Drain events SHOULD additionally include the
191	`run-id` of the execution attempt in `body` or in a structured `extra`
192	field.
193	
194	## 5. Outcome → Label / Event / Extra Mapping
195	
196	When a drain attempt finishes, `execute-bead` returns one of a fixed set of
197	outcomes. The mapping below is normative.
198	
199	| Outcome | Status transition | Label changes | Events appended | Extra updates |

194	## 5. Outcome → Label / Event / Extra Mapping
195	
196	When a drain attempt finishes, `execute-bead` returns one of a fixed set of
197	outcomes. The mapping below is normative.
198	
199	| Outcome | Status transition | Label changes | Events appended | Extra updates |
200	|---|---|---|---|---|
201	| `merged` | `in_progress → closed` | remove `claimed`, add `last-merged-rev:<sha>` (optional) | `closed-merged` | `Extra["last-run"]` updated with run-id |
202	| `already_satisfied` | `in_progress → closed` | (none) | `closed-already-satisfied` | `Extra["last-run"]` |
203	| `review_block` | `in_progress → blocked` | add `needs_human` (label) | `review-block` | `Extra["last-review"]` carries findings ref |
204	| `execution_failed` | `in_progress → open` (claim released) | (none) | `unclaimed` + a structured failure event | `Extra["last-run"]` |
205	| `no_changes_decomposed` | `in_progress → closed` | add `decomposed` | `no_changes_decomposed` | `Extra["children"]` lists child IDs |
206	| `no_changes_blocked` | `in_progress → blocked` | add `needs_human` | `no_changes_blocked` | `Extra["last-rationale"]` |
207	| `no_changes_no_evidence` | `in_progress → open` | add `triage` | `no_changes_no_evidence` | (none) |
208	| `no_changes_recoverable` | `in_progress → open` | (none) | `no_changes_recoverable` | (none) |
209	
210	`needs_human` is a **label**, not a status. It is the standard signal that
211	a human operator should look at the bead before drain re-attempts it.
212	
213	## 6. Claim Semantics
214	
215	Claim is metadata, not status. The persisted-status convention is that
216	`in_progress` implies a claim is held; `open` implies no claim. The actual
217	claim fields are:
218	
219	- `Extra["assignee"]` (string) — the entity holding the claim.
220	- `Extra["claimed-at"]` (RFC3339 timestamp) — when the claim was acquired.
221	- `Extra["claimed-pid"]` (int) — process id of the claim holder, advisory.
222	
223	Acquired:
224	
225	- During `open → in_progress` transition under the store lock.
226	- The drain loop and operator CLI (`ddx bead update --claim`) are the two
227	  legitimate drivers.
228	
229	Released:
230	
231	- Explicit `--unclaim` (or implicit unclaim on `in_progress → open`).
232	- On terminal close (`closed` / `cancelled`) the claim metadata is left in
233	  place as historical record but is no longer authoritative.
234	
235	Stale claim handling (TriageContract owns the policy):
236	
237	- A claim is *stale* if `claimed-at` is older than the configured stale
238	  threshold AND the bead is still `in_progress`.
239	- Stale claims are released by the auto-triage path (`auto-triage` event),
240	  status moves `in_progress → open`, and the bead becomes queue-eligible
241	  again.
242	- Auto-triage MUST NOT delete prior claim metadata; it appends an event
243	  recording the release.
244	
245	## 7. Naming-Role Decision Matrix
246	
247	Every name observed in code, schema, docs, or persisted data is assigned
248	a single category here. Names not in the persisted-status set MUST NOT
249	appear as `status` values.
250	
251	| Name | Category | Rationale |
252	|---|---|---|
253	| `open` | Persisted status | bd/br canonical; queue-eligible default. |
254	| `in_progress` | Persisted status | bd/br canonical; implies an active claim. |
255	| `closed` | Persisted status | bd/br canonical; terminal-success path. |
256	| `blocked` | Persisted status | bd/br canonical; hard precondition unmet. |
257	| `proposed` | Persisted status | bd/br canonical; pre-triage backlog. |
258	| `cancelled` | Persisted status | bd/br canonical; terminal-abandoned path. |
259	| `done` | Removed alias | Historical alias of `closed` in code paths. Not a persisted status. The reconciliation sibling bead replaces remaining code references with `closed`. |
260	| `pending` | Derived queue category | Used by queue derivation to mean "open AND has unmet deps" (a synonym for unsatisfied dependencies). Never persisted. The reconciliation sibling bead either renames it to `waiting` or folds it into `blocked` derivation. |
261	| `ready` | Derived queue category | "open AND no unmet deps AND not claimed". Computed from status+deps+claim; never persisted. |
262	| `review` | Terminal phase / event | A *phase of work*, not a status. Implemented as the `review-block` / `review-pass` event pair plus the `needs_human` label. Never persisted as a status. |
263	| `needs_human` | Label | Signals "an operator must intervene". Stays a label per bd/br compatibility — never a status. |
264	| `needs_investigation` | Label | Signals "the cause is unclear; triage required". Stays a **label** per bd/br compatibility. The reconciliation sibling bead removes any code path that treats it as a status. |
265	| `blocked-on-upstream:<id>` | Label | Parameterized label naming the upstream blocker. Distinct from the `blocked` status: a bead can carry the label while remaining `open` for visibility, or while `blocked` for enforcement. |
266	| `decomposed` | Label | Set by drain on `no_changes_decomposed`; pairs with `Extra["children"]`. |
267	| `triage` | Label | Set by drain on `no_changes_no_evidence` or by auto-triage. |
268	| `idle` / `draining` / `paused-quota` / `paused-rate-limit` / `exiting` | Worker state | Lives in the drain loop process, not on the bead. See section 10. |
269

1. When the drain loop receives NoViableProviderForNow, do NOT mark the bead as failed
2. Release the bead's claim so other workers (different machines, different provider config) can pick it up
3. Sleep until error.retry_after (with bounded max — default 30 min, configurable via --quota-pause-max)
4. On wake: probe by attempting the next ready bead. If still NoViableProviderForNow, sleep again with backoff
5. If retry_after is far in the future (> --quota-pause-max), exit the worker with a clear 'quota exhausted, expected recovery <timestamp>; relaunch when ready' message — operator decides when to relaunch
6. Per-worker emit a structured event ('drain-paused-quota') visible in ddx server workers list so operators can see paused workers

311	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
312	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
313	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
314	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
315		if exitCode != 0 {
316			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
317		}
318	
319		switch outcome {
320		case "merged":
321			// A merged outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker
322			// means the local target branch advanced but `git push` rejected the
323			// new tip (large blob, branch protection, auth, divergence, etc.).
324			// Classify these as push_failed so the loop refuses to close the
325			// bead and operators are forced to investigate.
326			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushConflictReasonPrefix) {
327				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
328			}
329			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushFailedReasonPrefix) {
330				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
331			}
332			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
333		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
334			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
335		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
336			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced
337		case "error":
338			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
339		case "preserved":
340			switch reason {
341			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
342				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
343			case "post-run checks failed":
344				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
345			case RatchetPreserveReason:
346				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
347			case OperatorCancelReason:
348				return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
349			default:
350				if isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason) {
351					return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
352				}
353				// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
354				// explicitly requested no merge.
355				return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
356			}
357		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
358			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
359		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
360			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
361		default:
362			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
363		}
364	}

369			"syntax sanity gate:",
370			"post-land gate failed:",
371		} {
372			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, prefix) {
373				return true
374			}
375		}
376		return false

383		case reason != "":

385		case errMsg != "":

// newTryCommand creates the top-level "ddx try" command: an operator-targeted
// one-shot dispatch of a single bead through the production execution
// machinery (claim → executor → land → review). It is the layer-2 primitive
// "ddx work" iterates over; reach for "try" when you need to force a specific
// bead through (deterministic ordering, debugging, comparison studies),
// reserve "work" for queue-priority drain.
func (f *CommandFactory) newTryCommand() *cobra.Command {
	cmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "try <bead-id>",
		Short: "Dispatch a specific bead through the execution machinery",
		Long: `try targets one specific bead by ID and dispatches it through the same
claim → executor → land → review path that "ddx work" iterates. It is the
operator-targeted layer-2 primitive: useful when you need deterministic
queue ordering (refactor execution, comparison studies) or when debugging
a single misbehaving bead under autonomous drain.

Pre-flight gates (fail-fast before any claim):
  - bead must exist
  - bead must be in a claimable status (open, or stale-in_progress)
  - bead must have all blocking dependencies closed

After the gates pass, try forces a one-shot iteration of the execute-bead
loop targeting only the specified bead. All routing flags (--harness,
--model, --profile, --provider, --effort) are forwarded as opaque
passthrough constraints — try does NOT validate them. The agent owns
routing within the requested bounds.

Exit code maps the bead's terminal status:
  0 — success or already_satisfied
  1 — preserved_for_review, no_changes, or any non-success non-failure
  2 — execution_failed (or pre-flight gate failed)
`,
		Example: `  # Dispatch one specific ready bead
  ddx try ddx-1a2b3c4d

  # Force a particular harness/model for debugging
  ddx try ddx-1a2b3c4d --harness codex --model gpt-5.4

  # Skip the post-merge reviewer
  ddx try ddx-1a2b3c4d --no-review`,
		Args:          cobra.ExactArgs(1),
		RunE:          f.runTry,
		SilenceErrors: true, // we manage stderr framing ourselves via cmd.PrintErrln
		SilenceUsage:  true, // pre-flight failures are not usage errors
	}

	// Routing knobs — passthrough only. Same surface as `ddx work`.
	cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
	cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
	cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
	cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
	cmd.Flags().String("profile", agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart")
	cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
	cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
	cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("opaque-passthrough", true, "Treat harness/provider/model as opaque (skip validation gate); default true for try")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("once", true, "Single-shot iteration (always true for try; flag retained for parity with work)")
	cmd.Flags().Duration("poll-interval", 0, "Unused for try (single-shot); flag retained for parity with work")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output result as JSON")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review")

11	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
12	// queue drain. Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness,
13	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
14	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and
25	--model as opaque passthrough constraints forwarded to the agent unchanged.
26	DDx does not validate these values or branch on them; the agent owns routing
27	within the requested power bounds.
28	
29	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
30	  drained     — no ready beads remain
31	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
32	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
33	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
34	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
35	
36	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
37	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
38	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
39	`,
40			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit
41	  ddx work
42	
43	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
44	  ddx work --once
45	
46	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
47	  ddx work --poll-interval 30s
48	
49	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
50	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
51	
52	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
53	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
54			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
55			RunE: f.runWork,
56		}
57	
58		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
59		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
60		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
61		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
62		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
63		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
64		cmd.Flags().String("profile", agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart")
65		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")

67		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")

70		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")

73		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review")

75		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")

81		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")

# Scope note — ddx-c6e3db02 execution

## Bead in scope

`ddx-c6e3db02` — per-bead rate-limit retry honoring Retry-After (provider stays in rotation).

## Out-of-scope build-fix sidecar

The base-rev `ef902f76` (chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead 20260503T203324-a13eca95)
does not compile. The merge `ff5e64ce` ("Merge preserved iteration ... after base
drift (ort -X ours)") preserved the `runtime.TargetBeadID` call site in
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:467` and the `formatTryResult` reference in
`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1934` from one branch of the `ddx try` work, while keeping the
other branch's `nextCandidate` signature (2 args) and never bringing in the
`formatTryResult` function. Result: `go build ./...` fails with:

```
internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:467:80: too many arguments in call to w.nextCandidate
        have (map[string]struct{}, string, string)
        want (map[string]struct{}, string)
cmd/agent_cmd.go:1934:10: undefined: formatTryResult
```

Without a building base, no test in `cli/internal/agent/` can run — including the
new `ratelimit_retry_test.go` this bead requires. Two minimal repairs were made
to unblock the build, both transparent to live behaviour:

1. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` — `nextCandidate` now accepts an
   optional variadic `targetBeadID ...string` parameter and applies it as a
   picker filter when set. This restores the dropped semantics from
   `ddx-f261d2a0` so the existing `runtime.TargetBeadID` call site type-checks
   without breaking the 2-arg call sites in `preview_queue_test.go`.
2. `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` — removed the dead `if tryTargetBeadID != ""` branch
   that called the missing `formatTryResult`. No production caller of
   `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl` passes a non-empty `tryTargetBeadID`; the live
   `ddx try` command lives in `cli/cmd/try.go` (preserved from commit
   `88dde5dc`) and runs its own dispatch path that does not touch
   `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl`. The branch was unreachable leftover from the
   alternate `ddx try` implementation in commit `5ce92857` that the merge
   discarded.

## Pre-existing test failures (not caused by this bead)

`go test ./...` still reports the following failures on the build-fix-only
baseline (verified by stashing the bead's changes and re-running):

- `cmd/TestReviewEvidenceApproveAttributesToTier` — a routing-evidence pipeline
  test that observes 0 review-outcome rows on the current `main`, independent
  of this bead.
- `cmd/TestAcceptance_US028..US034` and `cmd/TestInstallationPerformance` —
  installation acceptance tests that require a built binary at
  `cli/build/ddx`, which is not produced inside the execution worktree.

The bead's own AC #9 (`cli/internal/bead/schema_compat_test.go` round-trip)
passes.

## Bead AC coverage

All nine acceptance criteria are satisfied; see commit message for the per-AC
mapping.

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(agent): per-bead rate-limit retry honoring Retry-After [ddx-c6e3db02]

Adds RateLimitRetryContract enforcement (TD-031 §8.4) at the per-bead dispatch
layer. Distinct from QuotaPauseContract (ddx-aede917d): a 429 / rate-limit
response from the harness means "slow down for a moment", not "out of quota
for the period" — the provider stays in rotation and only the current bead
waits.

New module cli/internal/agent/ratelimit_retry.go:

- EvaluateRateLimitWait pure-function policy: honours Retry-After when
  present, falls back to the 1s/5s/15s/30s/60s exponential schedule
  otherwise, clamps each wait at the per-wait cap (60s default), trims
  the final wait so total elapsed never exceeds the budget.
- IsRateLimitResult detector that distinguishes 429-style throttling
  from quota-exhaustion wording (the latter belongs to QuotaPauseContract
  per AC #8 and is explicitly excluded).
- ParseRetryAfter / ExtractRetryAfterFromStderr surface the upstream
  Retry-After value from harness stderr lines.
- RunWithRateLimitRetry wraps a per-attempt closure with the retry loop;
  on budget exhaustion the final Result.Error is rewritten to
  "rate-limited beyond budget" so the standard execution_failed mapping
  fires (TD-031 §5) — no new bead status, persisted enum stays at the
  bd/br canonical 6 (ADR-004 preserved, schema_compat_test green).
- BuildRateLimitRouteAttempt builds a fizeau.RouteAttempt with
  Status="rate_limited" so RecordRouteAttempt records the event for
  routing-engine transparency without flipping provider availability.

Wiring (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go):

- ExecuteBeadRuntime grows a RateLimitMaxWait knob (zero = default 5 min,
  negative = disabled).
- ExecuteBeadWithConfig wraps dispatchAgentRun with RunWithRateLimitRetry.
  OnRetry calls Service.RecordRouteAttempt and appends a kind:rate-limit-retry
  event on the bead's stream per TD-031 §4 / §8.4.

CLI:

- cli/cmd/work.go gains --rate-limit-max-wait (default 5m).
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go forwards the flag through ExecuteBeadRuntime.

Tests (cli/internal/agent/ratelimit_retry_test.go) cover both AC #7 paths:
- Retry-After respected (TestRunWithRateLimitRetry_RetryAfterRespected,
  TestEvaluateRateLimitWait_RetryAfterRespected, *_ClampedByPerWaitCap).
- Exponential-backoff fallback
  (TestRunWithRateLimitRetry_ExponentialBackoffWhenRetryAfterAbsent,
  TestEvaluateRateLimitWait_ExponentialBackoffFallback).
Plus budget exhaustion → execution_failed mapping, non-rate-limit pass-through,
provider/harness/model propagation into RouteAttempt, context cancellation,
and the quota-wording exclusion that keeps QuotaPauseContract distinct.

AC mapping:
1. RunWithRateLimitRetry retries on IsRateLimitResult per TD-031 §8.4.
2. Wrapper does not mutate persisted bead status pre-budget-exhaustion;
   only Result.Error is rewritten on exhaustion, which routes through the
   standard execution_failed path. No new status added; bd/br compatibility
   preserved (schema_compat_test passes).
3. ParseRetryAfter / ExtractRetryAfterFromStderr honour Retry-After;
   rateLimitBackoffSchedule is the named fallback.
4. work.go --rate-limit-max-wait wired through to RateLimitRetryConfig.Budget.
5. Beyond budget: Error = RateLimitBudgetExhaustedReason flows to
   ClassifyFailureMode (auth_error → execution_failed). No new persisted
   status.
6. RunWithRateLimitRetry.OnRetry calls Service.RecordRouteAttempt with
   BuildRateLimitRouteAttempt(info).
7. TestRunWithRateLimitRetry_{RetryAfterRespected,ExponentialBackoffWhenRetryAfterAbsent}.
8. IsRateLimitResult excludes quota wording / NoViableProviderForNow.
9. cli/internal/bead/schema_compat_test.go round-trip passes.

Sidecar: a build break inherited from the base-rev's failed merge of
`ddx try` (missing `formatTryResult`, mismatched `nextCandidate` arity)
is repaired with two minimal edits so the package compiles. See
.ddx/executions/20260503T203324-a13eca95/scope-note.md for details.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -20

Commit landed with all pre-commit checks green. The bead is implemented:

**What changed:**
- New `cli/internal/agent/ratelimit_retry.go`: pure-policy module with `EvaluateRateLimitWait`, `IsRateLimitResult`, `ParseRetryAfter`, `ExtractRetryAfterFromStderr`, `RunWithRateLimitRetry`, `BuildRateLimitRouteAttempt`.

- `cmd/work.go`: `--rate-limit-max-wait` flag (default 5m).
- `cmd/agent_cmd.go`: forwards the flag through to `ExecuteBeadRuntime`.
- Tests in `ratelimit_retry_test.go` cover Retry-After-respected, exponential-backoff fallback, budget exhaustion, non-rate-limit pass-through, context cancellation, route-attempt provenance.
